title: Yosys can't parse port renames in module declaration
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

This file compiles successfully in vivado
```verilog
module foo (
         .another_name_for_a(a),
	 .another_name_for_b(b),
	 c
);

input a;
input b;
output c;

assign c = a ^ b;

endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

I would expect to parse correctly, as specified in the verilog grammar:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/148607/169949691-c093959e-90a3-4714-b89d-3fc70d5fc403.png)
(source: https://www.verilog.com/VerilogBNF.html)
and giving the following elaborated result (this is from Vivado):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/148607/169949772-8953d75d-737c-45e7-ac8b-dd9517a534b8.png)


## Actual behavior
```
 Yosys 0.17+41 (git sha1 c525b5f91, gcc 10.3.0-1ubuntu1~20.04 -fPIC -Os)

yosys> read_verilog module-decl-rename.v 
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: module-decl-rename.v
Parsing Verilog input from `module-decl-rename.v' to AST representation.
module-decl-rename.v:2: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_ID, expecting '.'
```
code_blocks:
Yosys 0.17+41 (git sha1 c525b5f91, gcc 10.3.0-1ubuntu1~20.04 -fPIC -Os)

yosys> read_verilog module-decl-rename.v 
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: module-decl-rename.v
Parsing Verilog input from `module-decl-rename.v' to AST representation.
module-decl-rename.v:2: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_ID, expecting '.'
